title: Idothea marginata
taxonomy: Idothea marginata is classified as a species within the genus Idothea and family Idoteidae. Idothea marginata is a type of isopod, specifically a marine woodlouse. The class to which Idothea marginata belongs is Malacostraca. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Its taxonomic classification helps scientists understand its position within the larger group of crustaceans.
uses: Idothea marginata, also known as the red beach-hopper, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Some studies suggest that extracts from the species may have antioxidant and antifungal effects, but these findings are still preliminary. The plant's traditional uses also include treating skin conditions and wounds, although this information is largely anecdotal. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand the medicinal potential of Idothea marginata.
